title: Hierarchical Structure of Word Classes in NLP
description: A flowchart illustrating the classification of words into major grammatical categories (parts of speech) and their subcategories, showing how words are analyzed from a raw token to a classified unit.
elements: Open Class Words (Nouns, Verbs, Adjectives, Adverbs)
Closed Class Words (Prepositions, Conjunctions, Articles, Pronouns)
relationships: Input is tokenized into individual words
Tokens are categorized into Open or Closed classes
Open classes are further subdivided into specific types
Closed classes are listed with their specific functions
